questions.question: What is the difference between a variable and a constant?
questions.answer: A variable can have its value changed after initial assignment. A constant is bound to a value that should not change. In Python, constants are a convention (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) with no runtime enforcement. In JavaScript, const prevents reassignment of the binding but not mutation of the object. In Java, final prevents reassignment.

questions.question: Explain Python's dynamic typing. How is it different from Java's static typing?
questions.answer: Python is dynamically typed: types are associated with objects, not variables. A variable can point to an int, then be rebound to a string — the interpreter checks types at runtime, not compilation. Java is statically typed: the variable's type is declared at compile time and never changes. Java catches type mismatches before execution; Python raises TypeError at runtime.

questions.question: What is the difference between == and is in Python?
questions.answer: == tests value equality (do two objects have equal content?). is tests identity (do two variables point to the exact same object in memory?). a = [1,2]; b = [1,2]; a==b is True but a is b is False. For singletons like None, always use is: if value is None.

questions.question: Explain the LEGB rule with an example.
questions.answer: LEGB = Local → Enclosing → Global → Built-in. Python searches for a name starting at the innermost Local scope, then the Enclosing function scopes (for nested functions), then the Global (module-level) scope, then Built-in names (print, len, etc.). Example: a function can access a global variable without declaring it global, but to modify it, must use the global keyword.

questions.question: Why is using a mutable default argument dangerous?
questions.answer: Default values are evaluated once when the function is defined — a mutable default (list, dict) is shared across all calls. If any call mutates it, later calls see the modified state. The fix: use None as default and create a fresh mutable inside the function body.

questions.question: How does Python garbage collection work?
questions.answer: CPython uses reference counting as its primary strategy. Every object has a refcount — the number of references pointing to it. When refcount reaches zero, memory is freed immediately. A cyclic garbage collector handles circular references (two objects pointing to each other) that reference counting cannot resolve, running periodically to find and collect these cycles.

questions.question: What are the performance characteristics of high-frequency Data Science Core Principles Part 2A queries?
questions.answer: It depends on the data structure used. Average queries execute in O(1) time when using hash indexes, but can degrade to O(N) linear search if colliding.
